notable_pick_trades: Traded 2026 4th-round pick to KC for Joe Thuney (2025). Traded 2026 6th to CLE for Joe Tryon-Shoyinka, received CLE 2026 7th. DJ Moore trade to BUF returns mid-round 2026 pick. Acquired 4th from LAR in 2025 draft.
roster_context: Coming off an 11-6 NFC North title and first playoff win since 2010 under first-year HC Ben Johnson. QB Caleb Williams broke franchise passing record with 3,942 yards and 27 TDs. Offense is set around Williams, but defense was propped up by turnovers and needs significant upgrades across all three levels. Cap is tight (~$5M under) with several costly veterans being moved. Drew Dalman's sudden retirement at 27 creates an unexpected IOL hole. Ozzy Trapilo's torn patellar tendon in the playoffs leaves LT barren. DJ Moore traded to Bills on March 5 for cap relief and draft capital. Tremaine Edmunds given permission to seek trade. Team is in win-now mode around Williams' rookie contract window.

needs.explanation: Interior defensive line was Chicago's weakest unit in 2025 — tied last in pass rush win rate (28.8%). Only Gervon Dexter (entering final year, 55.8 PFF grade) and aging Grady Jarrett (33 in April, disappointing first year) are under contract. Shemar Turner (2025 2nd-round pick) tore his ACL as a rookie and is uncertain for 2026. The Bears desperately need a disruptive 3-technique.
needs.depth: Shemar Turner (ACL recovery), Joe Tryon-Shoyinka — extremely thin

needs.explanation: Montez Sweat led the team in sacks but Chicago tied for dead last in pass rush win rate. There is no viable second edge rusher opposite Sweat. Austin Booker is a rotational piece. The Maxx Crosby trade pursuit is the Bears' biggest offseason storyline — they are the betting favorites to land him. If a Crosby trade doesn't materialize, EDGE becomes the top draft priority or a FA signing (Trey Hendrickson, Khalil Mack).
needs.depth: Joe Tryon-Shoyinka, DeMarcus Walker — no consistent second starter

needs.explanation: Left tackle is a crisis. Ozzy Trapilo (2025 2nd-rounder who emerged as the starter) suffered a torn patellar tendon in the wild card game and will miss most of 2026. Braxton Jones (UFA, was benched in Week 5) may re-sign cheaply as a bridge. Theo Benedet is a UDFA not viewed as a long-term answer. Kiran Amegadjie (2024 3rd-rounder) spent all of 2025 on IR. OL coach Roushar said the LT answer requires 'draft capital or an asset we currently don't have.'
needs.depth: Kiran Amegadjie (oft-injured), Ozzy Trapilo (out most of 2026)

needs.explanation: Jaylon Johnson (signed through 2027) played only 7 regular-season games due to groin injuries and looked a step slow. Tyrique Stevenson is inconsistent but had lowest passer rating allowed (86.3). Kyler Gordon (signed through 2028) is excellent at nickel when healthy but played only 3 regular season games in 2025. Nahshon Wright (UFA) may leave. Seven different CBs started at least one game in 2025. Adding a CB2 to play opposite Johnson would improve depth, but not as urgent as safety.
needs.depth: Kyler Gordon (nickel, injury-prone), Zah Frazier (2025 5th-rounder, missed rookie year)

needs.explanation: Neville Gallimore (2yr/$12M) replaces Andrew Billings as the nose tackle. He is a run-stuffer who adds durability but his AAV ($6M) is at the floor of the starter-tier market. Gervon Dexter and Shemar Turner provide interior depth. The immediate hole is patched but the unit lacks a true disruptive interior presence. IDL remains Tier 2 — this is still a draft-worthy position.

Drew Dalman's shocking retirement at 27 after a Pro Bowl season creates an immediate hole at center. Guards are elite (Joe Thuney, Jonah Jackson), but center is now vacant. Bears hosted FA Tyler Biadasz for a visit and are exploring options. Could be addressed via FA or mid-round draft pick.
needs.depth: Jordan McFadden, Luke Newman — no proven center on roster

needs.explanation: The Bears' top four safeties from 2025 are all unrestricted free agents: Kevin Byard (32), Jaquan Brisker (26), C.J. Gardner-Johnson (28), and Jonathan Owens. The entire safety room must be rebuilt. ESPN's Jordan Reid called safety 'arguably the Bears' top need.' Even if one is re-signed, Chicago needs at least one high-caliber starter via draft.
needs.depth: C.J. Gardner-Johnson (UFA), Jonathan Owens (UFA), Elijah Hicks (UFA) — literally no safeties under contract

needs.explanation: DJ Moore was traded to the Bills on March 5, leaving Rome Odunze as WR1 and Luther Burden III as WR2. Jahdae Walker showed flashes as a rookie. Colston Loveland is a receiving TE. The young core is promising, but WR depth behind the top two is thin. A Day 2-3 WR with big-play ability would round out the room.
needs.depth: Jahdae Walker, Colston Loveland (TE) — thin at WR3 after Moore trade

needs.explanation: Tremaine Edmunds has been given permission to seek a trade, which will save ~$15M in cap. T.J. Edwards (turning 30) broke his fibula in the playoff win over Green Bay and faces recovery. Noah Sewell tore his Achilles. D'Marco Jackson (UFA) impressed in spot starts. LB room is decimated by injury and cap-driven departures. Bears are 'firmly in the market for off-ball linebackers.'
needs.current_starter: T.J. Edwards (recovering from broken fibula), Tremaine Edmunds (trade candidate)
needs.depth: Ruben Hyppolite II (2025 4th-rounder), Noah Sewell (Achilles recovery)

scheme_fit_notes: Dennis Allen's 4-3 defense values interior disruption and a physical secondary. The defense was turnover-dependent in 2025 but tied for last in pass rush win rate, making DL and EDGE premium needs. Allen's system needs a true 3-technique DT who can penetrate and a rangy, ball-hawking safety. Ben Johnson's offense is built around play-action and creative schematic designs — the passing game should sustain with Odunze, Burden, and Loveland even after Moore's departure. LT must be able to handle speed rushers to protect Williams in Johnson's dropback concepts.
